THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY

PROCLAMATION .
To the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
The State for a number of years has faced a contin-
ually increasing deficit in the Treasury , and there are
now outstanding and unpaid , several millions of State
warrants bearing interest at five per cent . per annum .
The people of this Commonwealth realizing the press-
ing need of a readjustment of their laws relating to rev-
enue and taxation upon modern lines , have twice voted
favorably on an amendment to their Constitution author-
izing such legislation . The last General Assembly adopted
a resolution providing for the appointment of a com-
mission to devise a new system of revenue and taxation
for the State to be submitted at a regular , or special
session to be called for that purpose . This commission
has conducted its investigation and formally made its
report in conformity to the provisions of the resolution .
In the rush of business at a regular session of the
Legislature , where a multitude of measures are intro-
duced and considered , it can hardly be hoped that so
vital and complex a question as the revenue and taxing
system of the State can be given the consideration it
properly deserves . The people of the State must real-
ize that the present condition of the fiscal affairs of the
State is unsound and demands immediate relief . It pre-
sents , in my opinion , an occasion within the meaning of
the Constitution authorizing the Governor in convening
the General Assembly in extraordinary session .
In consideration of these conditions , and in accord-
ance with the power vested in me to convene the Gen-
eral Assembly on extraordinary occasions , I , as Gover-

nor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , issue this , my
proclamation , convening you in extraordinary session
at the seat of government in Frankfort , at twelve
o'clock noon , on Wednesday , February the fourteenth ,
nineteen hundred and seventeen , for the sole purpose of
considering the subject of revenue and taxation . You
will , therefore , in accordance with this proclamation , as-
semble in such session at the State Capitol at Frankfort
on the day and date above mentioned , to consider the
subject of revenue and taxation , exclusively .
Given under my hand , this , the third day of February ,
nineteen hundred and seventeen , and in the one hun-
dredth and twenty -fifth year of the Commonwealth .
A. O. STANLEY .( Signed )
By the Governor :
JAMES P. LEWIS ,
Secretary of State .
By J. A. SCOTT ,
Assistant Secretary of State .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GEN-
ERAL ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY
OF FRANKFORT , KENTUCKY , ON WEDNESDAY , FEB-
RUARY THE FOURTEENTH , NINETEEN SEVENTEEN ,
AND ENDED ON WEDNESDAY , APRIL THE TWENTY-
FIFTH , NINETEEN SEVENTEEN .
CHAPTER 1 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , creating a State Tax
Commission , providing for the appointment , qualification and
term of office of its members , prescribing its powers and
duties , appropriating money therefor , providing for the assess-
ment of property for taxation and the supervision and equali-
zation of assessments and prescribing the powers and duties
of county clerks , county assessors , sheriffs , collectors , the Rail-
road Commission , Auditor of Public Accounts and other officers
performing some duty in reference to the taxation of property ,
creating county boards of supervisors , a State Board of
Equalization and a State Board of Valuation and Assessment
and defining their powers and duties , prescribing penalties and
repealing existing statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Subdivision I.
Membership and Organization of Tax Commission .
(Sections 1-10 .)
§ 1. Membership and Qualification . There shall be
a State Tax Commission composed of three Commission-

4 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ers , two of whom shall be appointed as provided in sec-
tion two , one from each of the two dominant political
parties in the State . The two appointed members shall
be electors of the State , freeholders and not less than
thirty years of age . The Auditor of Public Accounts
shall be the third member of the State Tax Commission .
§ 2. Appointment and Term . The two Commission-
ers , other than the Auditor , shall be appointed by the
Governor by and with the advice and consent of the Sen- .
ate . Of the two persons first appointed , one shall be
appointed and designated to serve for a term ending on
the first day of July, 1918 , and one for a term ending
on the first day of July , 1919. Each of said terms shall
begin upon the qualifications of the persons appointed
therefor . Upon the expiration of the terms of the two
Commissioners first to be appointed , as aforesaid , each
succeeding Commissioner shall be appointed and hold
his office for the term of four ( 4 ) years , and each Com-
missioner shall hold his office until his successor shall
have been appointed and qualified . The Commissioners
shall be eligible for reappointment . They shall , during
their term of office , reside at the State Capital . No per-
son having been appointed and having served as a mem-
ber of the Tax Commission shall be eligible for any elec-
tive office in the State for a period of four years after
he ceased to serve as Commissioner , nor shall his name
be printed upon the ballot under the device of either
of the dominant parties as a candidate for any office .
§ 3. Removal. The Governor may at any time re-
move any appointive Commissioner for inefficiency , neg-
lect of duty , malfeasance in office , pernicious political
activities, or continued ill -health incapacitating him
from the performance of his duties , but before removal ,
the Governor shall furnish the Commissioner with a
copy of the charges against him, and shall designate a
date , not sooner than ten ( 10 ) days thereafter , when he

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY . 5
may appear in person and by counsel before the Gover-
nor and be heard in defense .
§ 4. Entire Attention to Commission . Each appoint-
ive Commissioner and each employe of the State Tax
Commission shall devote his entire time to the duties of
his office , and shall not hold any other position of trust
or profit , or engage in any other occupation or busi-
ness to which he is required to devote his personal at-
tention , or serve on any committee of any political party
during his incumbency of the office .
Any appointive Commissioner or employe of the Com-
mission who shall engage in pernicious political activity ,
or who shall in any manner solicit , demand , coerce , or
attempt to coerce , the contribution of money or any
other thing of value to any person for election purposes ,
or who shall influence or attempt to influence any legis-
lation through the instrumentality of his office or posi-
tion , except as otherwise in this act provided , shall be
removed from office or position by the Governor if the
guilty party be a member of the Commission , or by the
Commission if an employe . Any person wilfully violat-
ing the provisions of this section shall be deemed guilty
of a misdemeanor and shall be fined in any sum not less
than one hundred dollars ($ 100.00 ) nor more than one
thousand dollars ( $ 1,000.00 ) for each offense .
§ 5. Salary . Each appointive Commissioner shall
receive a salary of thirty -six hundred dollars ( $3,600.00 )
per annum , payable monthly as other State salaries are
paid . The Auditor , for his services as a member of the
Tax Commission , shall receive six hundred dollars per
annum , which sum shall be in addition to his salary as
Auditor .
§ 6. Employes and Expenses . The State Tax Com-
mission is authorized to employ a secretary and other
assistants and to fix their compensation . The salary of
the secretary shall not exceed two thousand dollars

6 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
($ 2,000.00 ) per annum . The Commissioners , secretary
and other assistants , that may be employed , shall be en-
titled to receive from the State their actual and neces-
sary expenses when traveling on business of the Com-
mission . Such expenses shall be submitted in an item-
ized claim as now provided by law, and the claim must
be approved by the Commission . The aggregate ex-
penses of the Commission for assistance , office expenses ,
and traveling expenses shall not exceed fifteen thousand
dollars ($15,000.00 ) per annum .
§ 7. Quorum. A majority of the Commission shall
constitute a quorum to transact business , and any va-
cancy shall not impair the right of the remaining Com- .
missioners to exercise all the powers of the Commission
so long as a majority remains . Any investigation , in-
quiry , or hearing , which the Commission is authorized
tò hold or undertake , may be held by or before any one
member of the Commission , and any decision or order
made pursuant thereto shall, if approved and confirmed
by the Commission , as shown on the records of the Com-
mission , be deemed the decision or order of the Commis-
sion .
§ 8. Seal . The State Tax Commission shall have an
official seal with such design as the Commissioners may
prescribe engraved thereon , by which it shall authenti-
cate its orders and proceedings .
§ 9. Quarters in Capitol . The custodian of public
buildings shall assign to the State Tax Commission suit-
able quarters in the Capitol Building and shall provide
the necessary office furniture . The Commission may
purchase the necessary office supplies . All such expenses
shall be audited and paid as the expenses of other State
offices are audited and paid .
§ 10. Sessions . The Commission shall be in contin-
uous session and open for the transaction of business
during all business hours of each and every day, except-

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY. 7
ing Sundays and legal holidays . The Commission shall
keep a record of its proceedings , which shall be a pub-
lic record . The Commission may hold sessions at any
place within the State ..
Subdivision II.
Powers and Duties of Tax Commission .
•
(Sections 11-13 .)
.
§ 11. Commission to Exercise Assessing Powers of
Other Boards . The State Tax Commission shall exer-
cise all the powers and perform all the duties with
reference to the assessment or equalization of the as-
sessment of property for purposes of taxation heretofore
exercised or performed by any State Board of Valuation
and Assessment , by the Railroad Commission , by the
State Board of Equalization , or by any other State Board
or Commission , or State officer , except the Revenue
Agents, Supervisor of Revenue Agents and the Insur-
ance Department in the office of the Auditor of Public
Accounts . And the State Tax Commission shall have
sole power to assess the physical or tangible value of
all property belonging to telephone corporations , operat-
ing and having exchanges or toll lines in more than three
counties of this State , whose franchise values they may
be required by this or any other law to assess for taxa-
tion , whether or not such physical or tangible values
have heretofore been assessed locally . The total assessed
valuation of such property , together with the franchise
value , shall be equalized with the assessed valuation of
similar classes of property throughout the State , and be
apportioned to the various taxing districts of the State
in the manner provided by section four thousand no hun-
dred and eighty-one of the Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's Edition , for the apportionment of the franchise of
telephone companies . For the purpose of ascertaining

8 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the tangible value of property , the commission shall re-
quire a report from such companies in such form and
containing such data as the commission may deem meet .
And all such powers and duties are hereby transferred
to the State Tax Commission to be exercised and per-
formed exclusively by it . It shall exercise such further
powers with reference to State taxes and assessments
by State authority as may be conferred upon it by law.
It shall be the duty of the Attorney General , when re-
quested by the State Tax Commission , to attend the
meetings of the Commission and advise with the same
in its proceedings .
§ 12. Specific Powers and Duties . It shall further
specifically be the duty of the State Tax Commission ,
and it shall have power and authority :
(1 ) Rules : To prescribe rules for its own government
and for the transaction of its business .
( 2 ) Oaths : Each Commissioner and the secretary of
the Commission is empowered to administer and certify
oaths .
(3 ) Forms : To prescribe , prepare and enforce the
use of forms for tax receipts in accordance with section
four thousand two hundred and thirty nine A , Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's Edition one thousand nine hundred
and fifteen-but they may change the size of the re-
ceipts , forms for listing property for assessment ,
forms of reports to be used as a basis for assessment or
equalization ; forms of tax books or assessment rolls ;
forms of recapitulation sheets , and forms of all other
reports required to be made or certified to the Tax Com-
mission or to any other taxing authority . A separate
form for listing all intangible personal property shall
be prescribed and furnished . The tax books or assess-
ment rolls prescribed and furnished shall provide for
valuing or stating all intangible personal property in
one total amount for the several items carried from the

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY. 9
form or schedule provided for listing such property . The
schedules to be used by the assessor and his deputies in
listing the property of taxpayers shall contain a list of
such separate items of property as may be prescribed by
the Commission , with interrogatories to be propounded
to each person by the assessor or his deputies and an
affidavit to be signed and sworn to by the party whose
property is assessed before the assessor or his deputies .
Said interrogatories shall be so framed as to elicit full
information concerning any property in the taxpayer's
custody or control of which another person is the legal
or equitable owner and full information as to any de-
vice used by the taxpayer to escape the payment of
taxes . It may also contain such inquiries for statistical
purposes as the Tax Commission deems proper . The
schedule shall contain two columns , in one of which the
person whose property is assessed shall fix the values ;
in the other , the assessor shall fix , after an examination ,
what , in his judgment , is the proper valuation of the
property . The party , or the assessor where the party
cannot write , shall insert the word " none " after each
item of the schedules provided for above when he has
no property to assess as named in such item , but no item
in the schedules shall be passed without being answered .
The assessor or his deputies shall read said affidavit in
full to each taxpayer and see that not (no ) item has
been passed unanswered before he shall be allowed to
sign or swear to it. Any person failing or refusing to
swear to said affidavit shall be deemed guilty of refus-
ing to give in his property for assessment . Any assessor
or deputies failing to administer the oath or certifying
falsely to the administering of the same or otherwise
violating the provisions of this section shall be fined not
exceeding fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) for each offense . Any
person who makes a false statement in listing for taxa-

10 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion his property or property in his custody or control
shall be guilty of false swearing .
Before the first day of September of each year the
State Tax Commission shall deliver to each County
Clerk for the use of the assessor and his deputies a suffi-
cient number of tax books and blank schedules for the
assessment of property in such county. The expense
of manufacturing and delivering said books and sche-
dules shall be audited and paid as other expenses of the
State offices , and shall not be considered an expense of
the Tax Commission as limited in Section 6 of this act .
(4 ) Witnesses : To summon and compel witnesses to
appear and give testimony , and to order and compel the
production of records , books , papers , and documents re-
lating to any matter which the Commission shall have
power to investigate or determine , in the same manner
in which like procedure is effected in circuit courts ; and
likewise to cause the depositions of witnesses residing
within or without the State or absent therefrom to be
taken upon notice to the interested party , if any. Pro-
vided that if any person , or persons shall fail or refuse
to obey the summons of the Commission to appear and
testify or to produce records , said failure shall be deemed
a misdemeanor and punishable by fine of not less than
twenty -five dollars or more than five hundred dollars .
The Commission may certify the facts to the Common-
wealth's Attorney , or the County Attorney , of the resi-
dence of the offending party , and it shall be the duty
of said Commonwealth's Attorney or County Attorney
to file an information against the offending party in the
circuit court and proceed against him as in other misde-
meanors . If, upon the trial of the case , it shall appear
that the offending party should comply with the order of
the Tax Commission , the court shall , in addition to
a fine , order the defendant to comply with such order of
the Commission .

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY. 11
(5 ) Examinations : To examine the books , accounts
and papers of the individuals , partnerships , companies ,
associations , and corporations , or to send its representa-
tive or agent to examine the same, when in the judgment
of the Commission such an examination being necessary
for the purposes of assessment and taxation .
(6) Advice to Assessing Officers : To confer with
and advise assessing officers , boards of supervisors and
other county officers as to their duties relative to taxa-
tion under the law , and supervise them in the perform-
ance of same .
(7) Prosecutions : To direct that proceedings , ac-
tions and prosecutions be instituted to enforce the laws
relating to the penalties , liabilities and punishment of all
persons , officers or agents of corporations , or others re-
quired by law to make returns of taxable property for
failure or neglect to comply with such provisions of the
tax law ; and to cause complaints to be made against as-
sessors , boards of supervisors , and other officers , whose
duties concern assessments , in any court of competent
jurisdiction for their removal for official misconduct or
neglect of such duty , as provided by law in such cases .
( 8) Commonwealth and County Attorneys to Assist :
To require the Commonwealth Attorneys and County
Attorneys to institute and prosecute actions and pro-
ceedings as provided in paragraph seven ( 7 ) imme-
diately above .
(9) Visit Counties : To visit the counties in the
State , when in their opinion it is necessary , for the in-
vestigation of the work and methods adopted by local
assessors , boards of supervisors and other taxing officials ,
in the assessment , equalization and taxation of property .
( 10 ) Convention of County Tax Assessors : It shall
be the duty of the State Tax Commission to arrange for
an annual conference for the county tax assessors of
each congressional district at some centrally located

12 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
place in said district for systematic instruction in find-
ing , and in the fair and just valuation and assessment
of every kind of property subject to taxation under this.
act , and as to their practical duties at every step in con-
nection therewith , said conference to continue not more
than two days . It shall be the duty of each county tax.
assessor to attend and take part in such conference un-
less prevented by illness , or for other reasons satisfac-
tory to the official conducting the conference . Any
county tax assessor wilfully failing to attend the con-
ference may be removed from office by the circuit court
of the county wherein he was elected . It shall be the
duty of each fiscal court to pay the actual and necessary
expenses of its county tax assessors in attending such
conferences , upon duly attested certificates of the State
Tax Commission , of actual attendance during the entire
period for which the conference is held , and that the
charges are reasonable .
( 11 ) Investigate Administration of Laws : To care-
fully investigate all cases where evasion of taxation of
property is alleged , complained of, or discovered , and to
ascertain wherein the existing laws are defective or im-
properly or negligently administered , and to take such
steps as may be necessary to correct the same.
( 12 ) Report : To transmit to the Governor , for trans-
mission to the legislature, at least thirty ( 30 ) days be-
fore each regular session of the legislature , a report
showing in statistical form the taxable property in the
State , the amount of taxes derived therefrom , together
with an itemized statement , under oath , of the expenses
paid by the Commission , giving the name and the
amount paid to each person , when paid , and what for,
and such other matters relating to revenues and taxa-
tion as shall be deemed of interest and value , together
with recommendations for the improvement of the sys-
tem of taxation for the consideration of the legislature .

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY . 13
§ 13. Information Confidential . It shall be unlawful
for any Commissioner , ex - Commissioner , any employe ,
member of County Board of Supervisors , agent , assessor ,
deputy assessor , or any other person , to divulge any in-
formation acquired by him of the affairs of any com-
pany , firm , corporation, person , association or co -part-
nership , or information regarding the schedules required
to be filed with the Tax Commission . Any violations of
the provisions of this section shall be a misdemeanor and
shall be punished by a fine not exceeding five hundred
dollars ($ 500.00 ) or by imprisonment not exceeding six
( 6 ) months , or both , and the guilty person shall be dis-
qualified and removed from office or employment in the
Commission . Provided , however , that this prohibition
shall not extend to information required in prosecutions
for making false reports or returns of property for taxa-
tion or any other infraction of the tax laws of the State .
Provided , further , that this prohibition shall not extend
to any matter required by law to be entered upon any
assessment book or roll .
Subdivision III .
Supervision and Equalization of Assessments .
(Sections 14-35 . )
§ 14. County Clerks ' Report of Sales . On or before
January first of each year , each County Clerk shall pre-
pare , on blanks which shall be furnished by the Tax
Commission , and shall file with the Tax Commission , a
tabulated statement of all sales of real estate as shown
by the deed books of his county for one year immediately
preceding the first day of September last , or for such
other period as the Tax Commission may require .
Said statement shall contain a list of all sales of real
estate , town lots being kept separate from other real
estate , the price paid, with terms and conditions , the

14 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
number of acres , and the assessed value of same for the
previous year. Where less than a fee simple is given ,
said statement shall show the interest conveyed ; and if
only a part of a tract of land or town lot is sold , it shall
give a short description of same and state what propor-
tion it bears to said tract or lot .
Said statement shall be sworn to by said clerk and
the county attorney and the latter in his affidavit shall
state that he has examined the recorded and unrecorded
conveyances filed for said time , and that each and every
conveyance with the consideration therefor and the as-
sessed values are correctly given in said statement .
For his services in making said tabulated statement
the clerk shall be paid a reasonable compensation by the
county court . If any clerk fails to file with the Tax
Commission on or before January first in any year the
tabulated list of sales , as provided in this act , he shall
be fined not less than fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) nor more
than two hundred dollars ( $200.00 ) and each day's de-
lay shall constitute a separate offense .
§ 15. Completion and Recapitulation of Assessor's
Books . After the assessor shall have completed his
work he shall return the tax books and schedules ; ex-
cept those schedules containing a list of the intangible
personal property , to the County Clerk or before the
first day of January, and the clerk shall file and pre-
serve the same ; the schedules shall be destroyed after
five years ; the schedules containing a list of the intan-
gible personal property shall be returned by the assessor
to the State Tax Commission on or before the first day
of January . The State Tax Commission shall examine
the schedules of intangible personal property , and shall
return to the County Board of Supervisors such sche-
dules as in its opinion are incorrect either by reason of
the omission or the undervaluation of such property .
After the County Supervisors have corrected the tax

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY . 15
books they shall return such schedules to the State Tax
Commission . The schedules shall be filed and preserved
by the State Tax Commission for a period of twelve
years , and shall not be public records except to the ex-
tent as provided under the provisions of this act . The
County Clerk shall test the accuracy of the extensions
and additions as shown by the tax books and make addi-
tions of each column on blanks to be furnished by the
Tax Commission , the footings on each page to show the
aggregate amount , value and number of each column in
said tax book, and record said capitulation in said tax
book with his official certificate attached . On or before
the second Monday in January , the County Clerk shall
file with the Tax Commission a certified copy of said
recapitulation .
§ 16. Tax Commission as Board of Equalization .
The State Tax Commission shall convene at the State
Capitol as the State Board of Equalization on the second
Monday in January in each year, for the purpose of
equalizing the assessment of property between counties .
At this session , the State Board of Equalization shall
carefully examine the recapitulation of the assessor's
books from each county , and shall compare it with such
data as they may obtain from any source , to determine
whether the assessments conform to law .
It shall have power to raise or lower the assessed
value of the property in any county. But it shall not
have power to raise or lower any individual assessment ,
after the same has been approved by the County Board
of Supervisors , except when a reassessment is made by
said Tax Commission as provided in Section 35 of this
act .
§ 17. Notice to Counties and Hearing . When it is
contemplated by the State Board of Equalization that it
will be necessary to raise the assessed value of property
in any county , it shall give notice of the contemplated

16 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
action to the county court and to the taxpayers of that
county in such manner as it shall deem sufficient , and
shall fix a day and place for a hearing . It may give such
information as it sees fit as to the reasons for the con-
templated increase and as to the classes of property
which in its opinion are improperly assessed . At said
time and place any taxpayer of the county, or any offi-
cer thereof , may appear and shall be heard . The county
court may appoint not to exceed three witnesses or ac-
credited representatives of the county to appear at such
hearings . The compensation and expenses of such wit-
nesses or representatives shall be paid by the county.
§ 18. Method of Equalization . Equalization shall
be accomplished by directing that the County Board of
Supervisors in revising the assessed value of all the prop-
erty or of any class of property in any county shall
make the total assessed value of all property or of each
of said classes of property amount to a given sum .
§ 19. Appointment of County Supervisors . The
County Judge shall, at the January term of court each
year, appoint three ( 3 ) intelligent , discreet housekeep-
ers and owners of real estate residing in different por-
tions of the county , who shall constitute the Board of
Supervisors of Tax in their respective counties . In coun-
ties containing cities of the first , second , third , fourth ,
fifth , and sixth class , at least one of said supervisors shall
be a resident of the largest city in the county.
20. Clerk to Copy Order of Appointment . The
clerk of the county court shall make as many copies
of the order appointing such supervisors as there are
supervisors appointed and one more , and deliver same
to the sheriff of the county, who shall deliver a copy to
each supervisor at least twenty ( 20 ) days before the
first day of March thereafter , and make due return
thereof to the clerk of the county court.
§ 21. Oath Supervisors Shall Take . The supervis-

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY . 17
ors , before they enter upon the discharge of their duties ,
shall take the following oath : " You swear that you will ,
to the best of your ability , discharge the duties required
of you as Supervisor of Tax , and that, in each instance
where the property has not been assessed at its fair cash
value , estimated at the price it would bring at a fair
voluntary sale , you will increase or decrease the value
and fix the value at what you believe the property would
bring at a fair voluntary sale ."
§ 22. Penalty for Failure to Attend Session- Va-
cancy . The failure to be in attendance promptly on the
day fixed for the session of the board to begin shall ,
without a reasonable excuse , subject the person or per-
sons so failing to a fine of not exceeding twenty -five dol-
lars ( $25.00 ) ; and the vacancy or vacancies so created ,
or from any other cause , shall be filled by the County
Judge .
§ 23. Time and Place of Meeting-Duty of Clerk .
The said supervisors shall convene at the county seat
on the second Monday of March . At the time of their
meeting , the county clerk shall deliver to said super-
visors the assessor's book for the county , together with
the order and recommendations of the Tax Commission ,
which shall be considered by said board , with other in-
formation , in ascertaining and fixing the value of prop-
erty in the county.
§ 24. Duty and Powers of Supervisors . The Board
of Supervisors shall make a careful examination of the
assessor's books , and each individual list thereof, and
may increase or decrease any list ; but the board shall
not reduce or raise any assessment unless the evidence
be clear and unmistakable that the valuation is not a
fair cash value , and shall list all property omitted by
the assessor which may be subject to taxation in the
county , and shall correct any errors of the assessor ; and
in cases where the property has not been correctly

18 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
valued , they shall fix the value thereof, and correct the
assessor's books , so as to show the true value of the
property , as herein provided ; but should any property
escape assessment by the assessors or supervisors , in
whole or in part , it may be assessed as provided in Sec-
tion 3 , Article 15 ( 4241 ) .
§ 25. Time of Sessions- Duties and Compensation of
Clerk . The board shall continue in session at its first
meeting in each year not less than two ( 2 ) nor more than
twelve ( 12 ) days , in counties not containing a city of
the first , second , third , fourth or fifth class ; in counties
having therein a city of the first class , the board may re-
main in session thirty (30 ) days ; in counties having there-
in a city of the second class , the board may remain in ses-
sion twenty -four ( 24 ) days ; and in counties having
therein cities of the third , fourth and fifth class , the
board may remain in session fifteen ( 15 ) days . The clerk
of the county court shall act as the clerk of the Board
of Supervisors . He shall be allowed the same compen-
sation for his services as a member of the board and be
paid in the same way . It shall be the duty of said clerk
to prepare and deliver to the sheriff a list of the names
of all persons whose property has been raised bythe
Board of Supervisors and the amount thereof .
§ 26. Notice to Taxpayers Raised -Compensation of
Sheriff . The sheriff shall notify all such taxpayers
whose list has been increased or assessed by the board,
and also notify them of the time to which the board ad-
journed .. Notice to non-residents or infants " or other
taxpayers temporally absent from the county at the
time for serving such notices , " shall be their attorney
or agent or guardian ; if none in the county, by posting
in some conspicuous place on the premises . The sheriff
shall be allowed a reasonable compensation for his serv-
ices , one -half to be paid out of State Treasury and one-
half to be paid out of the county levy .

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY . 19
§ 27. Reconvening to Hear Complaints - Witnesses .
The board, in reassembling , shall hear all complaints
and pass upon the assessment of all taxpayers , and for
that purpose the board, in counties having therein a
city of the first or second class , shall remain in session
not less than one ( 1 ) nor more than ten ( 10 ) days , and
in other counties not less than one ( 1 ) nor more than
five ( 5) days . The board may summon and swear wit-
nesses , and require them to testify . Any person who
shall wilfully fail to obey the summons of the board , or
shall refuse to testify before it when required , shall be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and , on conviction, be
fined not less than twenty -five ( 25 ) nor more than one
hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) for each offense .
§ 28. Assessment of Property , Owner Not Known .
If the board, during its session , find any property which
has not been assessed , and for which they can find no
owner , they shall describe and locate such property and
assess the value thereof.
§ 29. Record Kept by Board . The Board of Super-
visors shall keep a record of their proceedings and cor-
rect the tax books thereby . They shall annex their cer-
tificate to the tax books that they have examined , and
approve the same, and the number of days they were
actually engaged , and shall return the same with the
record of their proceedings , to the county clerk on or
before the first day of May .
§ 30. Clerk to Certify to Court-Payment of As-
sessor , Supervisors and Clerk . The clerk shall certify
to the county court the approval of the tax books , and
the county court shall enter the fact of record ; also the
amount due the supervisors for their services . On a
presentation of a copy of the order, the Auditor shall
draw his warrant on the Treasurer in favor of the as-
sessor for the portion of his claims , as provided by law ,
and the amount due the supervisors and clerks for their

20 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
services . And for his services in making out the tax
bills now required by law to be made and delivered to
the sheriff the county clerk shall receive five cents each
to be paid in the same manner now provided .
§ 31. Compensation of Supervisors and Clerks- Pay-
ment of. The supervisors and clerks shall be allowed
three dollars ($3.00 ) per day for each day they may be
necessarily employed , to be paid one -half by the fiscal
court of the county and the other half out of the State
Treasury .
§ 32. Errors Do Not Invalidate Assessment -Ap-
peal . Any informality or irregularity in the execution
of their duties as supervisors , and any failure of duty
on their part , shall not render any assessment invalid .
But any taxpayer feeling himself aggrieved by the action
of said Board of Supervisors may appeal to the quar-
terly court within thirty days after the final adjourn-
ment of said board , by filing with the judge of said
court a certified copy under the hand of the clerk of
said board of the action of said board . He shall have
the right to appeal from the decision of the quarterly
court to the circuit court and then to the Court of Ap-
peals in the same manner that the law now allows ap-
peals in civil cases. It shall be the duty of the county
attorney to represent the board in all such suits pend-
ing before the quarterly or circuit court, and it shall be
the duty of the Attorney General to represent the board
in the Court of Appeals . When any such appeal is
taken it shall be the duty of the county attorney to
forthwith notify the State Tax Commission in writing
of such appeal , and if he fails to so notify the State
Tax Commission within ten days after such an appeal
has been taken to the quarterly court he shall be guilty
of a misdemeanor and upon conviction may be fined
in any sum not to exceed one hundred dollars . If the
County Attorney or Sheriff feel that the State , county

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY. 21
or any taxing district has been aggrieved by the action
of the board of supervisors , or on account of the failure
of said board to act , then either of said officers may
appeal to the quarterly court by filing with the judge
thereof a brief statement showing the action of the
Board of Supervisors , or stating wherein it failed to
properly act . The quarterly court shall hear evidence
and pass upon all such appeals and either of said offi-
cers or Tax Commission may appeal to the circuit court
and then to the Court of Appeals the same as in other
civil cases . It shall be the duty of the County Attorney
to represent the interest of the State and county in all
such appeals in the quarterly and circuit court, and it
shall be the duty of the Attorney General to represent
the interest of the State and county in the Court of
Appeals . Where an appeal is taken by the County At-
torney or Sheriff as herein provided , it shall be the duty
of the County Attorney to notify the State Tax Com-
mission in like manner as when an aggrieved taxpayer
appeals , and if he fails so to do he shall be subject to a
like penalty.
�
§ 33. Recapitulation of Supervisors ' Work . The
County Clerk shall after the examination and approval
of the tax book by the Board of Supervisors make and
record a recapitulation of the tax book as revised by the
Supervisors , in the same manner provided in Section 15
of this act for the recapitulation of the tax book as
prepared by the assessor . On or before May 10th the
County Clerk shall file with the Tax Commission a certi-
fied copy of said recapitulation .
§ 34. Commission to Certify to Auditor - Duties of
Auditor . If it appear from said recapitulation that the
Board of Supervisors have complied with the orders of
the Tax Commission , the Commission shall, not later
than June first , approve the action of such Board of
Supervisors and certify to the Auditor of Public Ac-

22 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
counts the total assessed value of the property in each
such county . The Auditor shall at once compute the
amount of taxes due from each such county , charge said
amount to the sheriff and shall execute and transmit to
the County Clerk a certificate stating that the tax books
as revised by the Supervisors have been finally ap-
proved . He shall at the same time furnish to the County
Clerk , in loose leaf form , ten per cent . more tax bills
and stubs than there are taxpayers in such county . Said
tax bills and stubs to be in the form prescribed by the
Tax Commission . The clerk shall affix said certificate
to the tax books and enter it of record in the order
book, and it shall be the sheriff's or collector's warrant
for the collection of taxes .
§ 35. Reassessments . In the event the County
Board of Supervisors fail or neglect to carry out the
orders of the State Tax Commission , the Commission
may itself make a reassessment and equalization of all
or any of the property of any such county and fix the
value thereof . The necessary expense of such reassess-
ment , not exceeding the amount allowed to the assessor
and supervisors for an original assessment in such
county, shall be borne by such county and shall be
ordered paid by the fiscal court upon the presentation
by the Tax Commission of a statement showing the
amount of said indebtedness . Immediately upon the
completion of such reassessment , the Tax Commission
shall certify to the Auditor the total assessed value of
the property in such county, and the Auditor shall pro-
ceed as provided in the preceding section of this act .
§ 36. Repealing Clause . The following sections of
the Kentucky Statutes (Carroll's 1915 Edition ) are
hereby repealed : Section 4058 , 4059 , Sections 4115 to
4128 inclusive , Section 4239 , Section 4239c , Section
4239g, and Sections 4268 to 4281 inclusive . All other

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY . 23
laws or parts of laws in conflict with this act are hereby
repealed to the extent of such conflict .
$ 37 . Emergency Clause . Whereas the present
State revenue is inadequate to defray the necessary ex-
penses of government , an emergency is declared to
exist and this act shall become a law upon its passage
and approval by the Governor ; Provided , however , the
assessment of property as of September 1st , 1916 , the
supervision and equalization of said assessment , and the
collection of taxes based upon said assessment as of Sep-
tember 1st , 1916 , shall be governed by the laws in force
on September 1st, 1916 , and the repeal of Sections 4268-
4281 , which relate to the State Board of Equalization
shall not take effect until July 1st , 1917 .
Approved April 11 , 1917 .
CHAPTER 2 .
AN ACT to provide revenue by taxation for the improvement and
construction of the public roads and bridges of the county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. [Road Tax-Order Calling Flection - Issual of
Bonds ] . The fiscal court of any county in the State
may submit to the voters at a special election to be
held for that purpose , the question of voting a tax
of any sum not exceeding 20 cents on the hundred dol-
lars on all property subject by law to local taxation ,
under Section 157a of the Constitution , for the im-
provement or construction of the public roads and
bridges of the county, either or both , as the fiscal court
may direct . The order of the fiscal court calling the
election shall specify the amount of the tax to be levied
each year and the number of years for which the tax
may be imposed , not exceeding ten years , and shall also
provide that no amount of money in excess of the

24 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
amount that can be raised by the levy in any one year
shall be expended in that year . It may also borrow
money in any year in advance of the collection of the
tax for that year, not exceeding 80 per cent . of the esti-
mated tax, and issue bonds therefor , the amount of the
tax to be estimated , according to the assessment and
collection of the preceding year, but any money so bor-
rowed shall be paid out of the money raised from the
tax in the year in which the money is borrowed . So that
all indebtedness created in any one year shall be paid
out of the fund raised in that year.
§ 2. [Amount of Tax ] . The fiscal court shall also
in the order calling the election specify the amount of
ad valorem tax to be levied each year, which shall be
any sum not exceeding 20 cents on the one hundred dol-
lars of assessed property .
§ 3. [How Question Submitted ] . The question to
be submitted to the people shall be : " Are you for a
property tax of cents on each one hundred dol-
lars ' worth of property in the county, to be levied each
year for years , for the purpose of improving or
constructing , either or both, the roads and bridges of
the county ?" The rate of taxation , and the number of
years for which the tax is to be levied to be inserted in
respective blank spaces , and two small squares to be
placed opposite the question and on the right of same ,
one for votes for and one for votes against the measure ,
designated respectively by the words "Yes ," and
" No." And if a majority of the voters voting upon
this proposition are in favor of it , then the fiscal court
shall, as soon as the result has been ascertained and
certified , make provision for the road and bridge work
contemplated .
§ 4. [ Collection ] . The sheriff shall collect the taxes
at the same time and in the same manner and by the
same processes that he collects other taxes and shall

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY. 25
pay the same over as the fiscal court may direct, and
shall be liable on his official bond for the faithful per-
formance of his duties hereunder . For the collection of
the tax levied hereunder the sheriff shall be entitled to
a commission of one per cent . of the amount so collected .
§ 5. [ Advertisement ] . The fiscal court shall direct
and it shall be the duty of the sheriff to advertise the
time and purpose of the election and the amount of tax
to be levied each year in the paper published in the
county having the largest circulation for thirty days
before the election ; and if there be no paper published ,
then by printed advertisement posted up at the court
house door and at three other public places in each pre-
cinct for thirty days before the election .
§ 6. [ Election Officers ] . The election above pro-
vided for shall be held by the same officers who hold
the regular election , and the election shall be held and
the returns thereof made in the same manner as is pro-
vided by law for the regular election .
§ 7. [Emergency ] . As there is no adequate system
or law by which counties may levy the tax herein pro-
vided , and the counties of the State being in need of
some immediate aid in this respect , an emergency is
hereby declared to exist , and this bill shall become ef-
fective upon its passage and approval .
Approved April 25 , 1917 .

CHAPTER 4 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , prescribing the man-
ner of taxing money on deposit in banks , prescribing by
whom said taxes shall be paid , fixing a rate for such taxation ,
exempting said deposits from local taxation , providing for cer-
tain reports by banks relative to said deposits , fixing the
time for said reports and the time and manner for the pay-
ment of said taxes , prescribing penalties and repealing exist-
ing statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. [ Tax on Bank Deposits ] . Every person , firm
or corporation of this State having, on the first day of
September , a deposit in any bank , trust company , or
combined bank and trust company , organized under the
laws of this State , or in any national bank in this State ,
shall pay a tax to the State which is hereby assessed at
the rate of one -tenth of one ( 1 % ) per cent . annually ,
upon the amount of such deposit so held by such bank
on the first day of September , and no deduction there-
from shall be made on account of any indebtedness . The
taxes imposed by this section shall be paid by such bank,
trust company and combined bank and trust company ,
or national bank, for and on behalf , and as agent , of
the depositor therein to the Auditor on or before the
first day of December next following the date whereon
the report provided for in section 3 is required to be
made .
§ 2. [No Other Tax Assessed ] . No other tax shall
be assessed on such deposits in bank or against the de-
positor of said deposits by the State or any county , city ,
town or other district .
§ 3. [Bank to Report and Pay Tax on Deposits ] .
Each bank , trust company , and combined bank and trust
company and national bank doing business in this
State , shall file, on or before the 21st day of September

28 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
in each year, with the Tax Commission a report setting
forth the total amount of its deposits as of September
first of each year, which would be taxable in the name
of the depositor , but excluding therefrom such deposits
as are not taxable against the depositor under the laws
of this State , and shall , on or before the first day of
December of each year, pay to the Auditor one -tenth of
one per cent . of the amount of such deposits and may
charge to and deduct from the deposit of each depositor
the amount of the tax so paid for and on his behalf and
a lien as of September first of each year is hereby given
to the banks and trust companies so paying the tax on
the funds belonging to each depositor , respectively , on
September first of each year and on which the tax shall
be so paid by them .
Any claim for taxes against the depositor charged to
or otherwise asserted against him by the bank paying
the taxes shall be so charged or asserted within six months
after payment of the taxes to the Auditor , and all claims ,
demands or lien therefor shall be thereafter barred .
§ 4. [Penalty for Failure to Report ] . Each bank
and trust company that wilfully fails to make the re-
turns , or to pay the taxes for and on behalf of its de-
positors provided for and in this act shall forfeit an
amount equal to one -tenth of one per cent . of such de-
posits to the use of the State for each month's delay in
filing such return , or in paying such tax . Such tax and
forfeitures may be recovered in an action on this stat-
ute commenced by any county attorney in the county
court of the county in which such bank or trust com-
pany may be located , but if the Tax Commission , or the
court wherein such action is heard for the recovery of
such acts , or forfeitures , becomes satisfied that such fail-
ure was not wilful , said Tax Commission , or said court
may , in its discretion waive any part or all , of such
penalty .

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY. 29
§ 5. All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Approved May 2, 1917 .

CHAPTER 5 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , and providing a
license tax on distilled spirits , known as double stamp spirits ,
providing for the distribution of said fund , and providing
penalties for the violation of its provisions .
-Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. [Tax on Distilled Spirits ] . Every corporation ,
association , company , co -partnership or individual en-
gaged in the business or occupation of manufacturing
distilled spirits , known as whiskey or brandy, or other
species of such double stamp spirits in this State , and
every owner or proprietor of a bonded warehouse in

every proof gallon of said distilled spirits which is li-

mission .
§ 2. [ Reports Payment and Distribution of Taxes ] .
Every corporation , association , co - partnership or in-

scribed above and every owner or proprietor or posses-
sor of bonded warehouse in which distilled spirits as
above described are stored , shall on the first day of Jan-
uary , May , and September , each year , make a report
to the Auditor of Public Accounts, in writing , sworn to
by the person making the report, or if the owner or

proprietor or possessor be a corporation , such report
shall be made and sworn to by its principal officer or the

gallons , the quantity of such spirits on which the gov-
ernment tax has been paid , or has become due , or trans-
fered under bond or removed from the warehouse dur-
ing the preceding four months , and shall , at the time
of making the report pay to the Treasurer , through the
Auditor , the aforesaid tax of two cents per proof gal-
lon on such distilled spirits reported on as above di-
rected . All distilled spirits liable to taxation under this
act now in bonded warehouses in this State will be sub-
ject to this tax, the same as such spirits which shall be
hereafter distilled , or stored in such warehouses , and

Fund thirty ( 30 ) per cent . thereof ; and fifty ( 50 ) per
cent thereof to the General Expenditure Fund of the
State .
§ 3. [ Report on May 1st , 1917 ] . It shall be the duty
of each firm , corporation, association , co -partnership or
individual engaged in the business of manufacturing
distilled spirits , or owner or proprietor of a bonded
warehouse , in the State to make a report on the first
day of May , one thousand nine hundred and seventeen ,
showing the total number of proof gallons of such dis-
tilled spirits as above described in each and every
bonded warehouse owned or controlled by such person ,
firm , corporation , association or individual , and at the
same time give the capacity of the distillery . This re-
port to be in addition to the report for license tax pay-
ing purposes as above provided .

CHAPTER 6 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , requiring an excise
tax to be paid on all beer , lager beer , ale , porter , and other
similar fermented liquors sold at retail in this State or sold at
wholesale to persons doing business in this State , providing
the manner of collecting said tax, requiring reports , and
fixing fines and penalties for violations of the act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Excise Tax on Fermented Liquor . There shall
be paid on all beer , lager beer , ale , porter and other sim-
ilar fermented liquors sold at retail in this State or sold
at wholesale to persons doing business in this State
a tax of ten ( 10 ) cents for every barrel containing not
more than thirty -one ( 31 ) gallons and a tax at a like
rate for any other quantity sold to be paid by the brewer
if he be in the State ; if not, by the brewer's agent , or
*

32 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the wholesaler if he be in the state ; and if neither of
those just mentioned be in the state then by the re-
tailer . Said tax shall be credited by the Auditor to the
General Expenditure Fund .
Every person or firm , and the principal officer of
every corporation engaged in the brewing and manu-
facture of such liquors shall, on or before the tenth of
each month render to the Auditor of Public Accounts
a sworn statement in writing , taken from his books , of
the quantity of such malt liquors sold or removed for
sale to persons doing business in this State and to per-
sons without the State during the preceding month and
shall at the same time pay into the State treasury the
tax on that part thereof sold within the State . Every
person , firm or corporation acting as brewer's agent , or
selling by wholesale or retail malt liquor manufactured
outside of this State shall on or before the tenth of
each month render to the Auditor a sworn statement of
the quantity so sold during the preceding month and
the names and addresses of the persons , firms or cor-
porations from whom purchased , and shall at the same
time pay into the State treasury the tax upon all malt
liquors so sold in the State upon which the tax has not
already been paid . For the purposes of the payment of
taxes under the provisions of this section , the entire
contents of a barrel or case shall be considered sold as
soon as any part thereof is sold and shall be reported
and the tax thereon shall be paid as of the month in
which same is opened . Any failure to make the reports
or pay the tax as provided in this section shall be a mis-
demeanor punishable by fine of not less than ten dol-
lars ( $ 10.00 ) nor more than twenty -five dollars ( $ 25.00 ) ,
and each day's delay shall constitute a separate offense .
§ 2. All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
§ 3. This act shall take effect August 1st, 1917 .
Approved May 2, 1917 .

CHAPTER 7.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , providing for corpora-
tion license taxes for certain corporations , providing amount
and manner of paying same , requiring reports to be made and
manner of making same , fixing fines and penalties for violation
and repealing existing statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. License Tax on Corporations . All corporations
having capital stock divided into shares , organized by or
under the laws of this or any other State or Govern-
ment , owning property or doing business in this State ,
except foreign insurance companies , whether fire , life ,
accident , casualty or indemnity , foreign and domestic
building and loan associations , banks and trust com-
panies , and all corporations which , under existing laws ,
are liable to pay a franchise or license tax , shall pay
to this State , to be credited to the sinking fund , an an-
nual license tax based upon its authorized capital
stock , as hereinafter provided .
§ 2. Classification . For convenience in classifica-
tion , corporations are divided into two classes , domestic
and foreign . A domestic corporation is one incorpor-
ated by and under the laws of this State ; every other
corporation is a foreign corporation .
§ 3. Amount of Tax . Domestic and foreign corpora-
tions shall pay an annual license tax of fifty cents on
each one thousand dollars of that part of their author-
ized capital stock represented by property owned and
business transacted in this State , which shall be ascer-
tained by finding the proportion that the property
owned and business transacted in this State bears to
the aggregate amount of property owned and business
transacted in and out of this State .
Provided , that such corporation may pay at said rate
upon their entire authorized capital stock ; and in that

34 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
event they shall not be required to report as in para-
graph c of Subsection 4 hereof. And their failure so to
report shall be deemed conclusive evidence that such
corporation elects to pay upon its entire authorized cap-
ital stock, and it shall be its duty so to do , and the duty
of the Board of Valuation and Assessment so to fix its
license tax .
§ 4. Reports to Commission -What Shall Show- As-
sessment by Board of Valuation .- In order to ascertain
the amount of taxes due and payable under the next
preceding section , by such corporations now owning
property or transacting business in this State , it shall
be the duty of every such corporation to file with the
Tax Commission on or before the first day of February ,
1918 , and on or before the same day annually thereafter ,
a written report , verified by the affidavit of the presi-
dent or secretary of such corporation , showing :
(a) The name of such corporation , the name of the
State or Government under the laws of which it is in-
corporated , the date of incorporation , the place of its
principal office in and out of the Commonwealth , the
name and postoffice address of its president and secre-
tary , the name and postoffice address of its authorized
agent or attorney upon whom process may be executed ,
as provided by law, and the name and address of its
officer or agent in charge of its business in this State .
( b ) The total amount of its authorized capital stock .
(c ) The value and location of the property owned
and used by the company in Kentucky and the value of
the property owned and used by the company in each
State or foreign country outside of Kentucky , the ag-
gregate amount of business transacted by said company
during the preceding year ending the 31st day of Dec-
ember , and the proportion of such business transacted
in this State and in each other State , or foreign coun-

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY. 35
try, and such other facts bearing on proper taxation as
the Tax Commission may require .
It shall be the duty of the Tax Commission acting as
a Board of Valuation and Assessment , from such re-
ports and from such additional information as it may
require, to ascertain and fix that part of the authorized
capital stock of such corporations upon which the license
tax shall be based , as herein provided , and to fix the
license tax of such corporations at the rate hereinbefore
prescribed . The board may in any case require such
additional information as it may deem necessary to en-
able it to perform its duties herein ; and it shall be its
duty to notify every such corporation of the amount so
assessed . The notice may be given as provided in Sub-
section 5 hereof, and it shall be the duty of the corpora-
tion to pay the amount of such tax to the Auditor of
Public Accounts not later than thirty ( 30 ) days there-
after or not later than thirty ( 30 ) days after final action
by the board, should it grant a rehearing , which the
board may grant upon application therefor , filed within
thirty ( 30 ) days after the date of such notice . Upon
final action by the board it shall certify to the Auditor
of Public Accounts the amount of such tax due from
each and every such corporation .
§ 5. Secretary of State to Report to Tax Commission
Name and Address of Corporation . It shall be the duty
of the Secretary of State to certify to the Tax Commis-
sion the names and addresses of all corporations coming
within the purview of this article , and also the names of
their designated agent upon whom process may be exe-
cuted , that now or that may hereafter appear upon the
records of his office .
From such certification and from the records of its
own office, it shall be the duty of the Tax Commission to
notify such corporations required to report under this
section and to furnish a blank upon which to make the

36 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
report , not later than December fifteenth in each year.
Proof that such notice and blank have been deposited
in the United States mail duly stamped and addressed
to the person last designated as the person upon whom
process may be served , shall in all cases be deemed con-
clusive evidence that such notice to report had been
given to such corporation .
§ 6. Domestic Corporations Exempt Year of Organi-
zation. Domestic corporations hereafter incorporated
shall not be required to pay the annual license tax in
this section provided for the year in which they may
be organized .
§ 7. Reports , When to be Made . Domestic corpora-
tions hereafter incorporated , and foreign corporations
hereafter becoming the owner of property or transact-
ing business in this State , shall make such report to the
Tax Commission in the manner provided in this section ,
on or before February first succeeding their incorpora-
tion or succeeding their becoming the owner of property ,
or transacting business , in this State ; and shall make
such report on or before said date annually thereafter .
And , except as provided in Subsection 6, such corpora-
tions shall pay the annual license tax at the rate and
in the proportion provided in Subsection 3 ; and in all
respects be subject to all the provisions and penalties in
this section contained and prescribed .
§ 8. Penalty for False Statements . Any person who
shall falsely make an affidavit herein required shall be
guilty of false swearing, and , upon conviction therefor ,
shall suffer the pains and penalties in such cases made
and provided . Any corporation which wilfully violates
the provisions of this section or any of such provisions ,
or wilfully fails or neglects to perform any duty herein
imposed upon it, shall be deemed guilty of a misde-
meanor and , upon conviction , shall be fined any sum not
less than fifty dollars nor more than one thousand dol-

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY . 37
lars for each offense ; the same to be recovered by penal
action or by indictment in the Circuit Court of Frank-
lin County ; and every such judgment of conviction for
failure to report or for failure to pay the annual license
tax required , shall also provide that the right to be a
corporation in this State , shall be suspended until the
fine and costs and all taxes and penalties due the State
shall be paid or replevied , or the judgment superseded .
Every corporation failing to pay its taxes as provided in
this section within the time herein prescribed shall be
deemed a delinquent , and a penalty of ten per centum
on the amount of such tax shall attach , and thereafter
such tax shall bear interest at the rate of ten per centum
per annum , the same to be collected in the same manner
and by the same process as the tax is collected .
§ 9. Minimum Fee . No corporation required to pay
an annual license tax under this section shall pay a less
sum therefor than ten dollars .
§ 10. Sections 4189a to 4192 , inclusive , Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1915 Edition , are hereby repealed .
All other laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict .

AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , and providing a
referendum on acts of the General Assembly in certain cases
where property is classified for taxation , and a lower rate
placed on personal property , tangible or intangible, than upon
real estate , providing the manner and means of taking the
sense of the people in such cases and fixing fines and penalties .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. [ Referendum of Act Classifying Property for
Taxation- Petition For ] . Whenever the General As-
sembly shall pass or enact any law pursuant to or under

38 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 171 of the Constitution as amended , classifying
property and providing a lower rate of taxation on per-
sonal property , tangible or intangible , than upon real
estate , the provisions of such act shall be subject to a
referendum upon the written petition of regularly quali-
fied legal voters equal in number to five per cent . of the
votes cast for the candidates for Governor at the last
preceding regular election . This shall apply to five per
cent . of the legal voters of the State and not of each
county .
Such petition shall be filed with the Secretary of State
not more than four months after the final adjournment
of the General Assembly which passed the bill on which
the referendum is demanded and shall specify the act ,
or the item , section or part of the act on which the
referendum is demanded . Each sheet of such petition
shall contain the names of legal voters in but one voting.
precinct , and shall state the county and precinct . Each
voter shall write his postoffice address opposite his name
and in cities of the first , second or third class , his street
address .
§ 2. [Genuineness of Signatures - How Determined ] .
Within five days after the filing of such petition the
Secretary of State shall transmit to the county court of
each county in which petitioners reside the sheets con-
taining the names of the petitioners of such county . The
county judge shall thereupon set a date not less than
ten nor more than fifteen days thereafter , when inter-
ested persons may appear and present proof as to the
genuineness of signatures and the qualification of peti-
tioners . After hearing proof , the county judge shall
determine the number of legal voters of his county who
have signed such petition , caused same to be entered
upon the order book and certify same to the Secretary
of State .
§3 . [Publication of Petition ] . Within thirty days

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY. 39
after receiving from county courts certification that a
number of legal voters have signed such petition consti-
tuting in the aggregate five per cent . of the vote of the
entire State as hereinbefore provided , the Secretary of
State shall cause such proposed law or laws or the item ,
sections , or parts thereof affected , to be published upon
at least four separate dates in two papers of general cir-
culation published in the State , and shall also cause to
be published at the same time and in the same manner
the fact that same will be submitted to the voters for
their acceptance or their rejection at the next general
election .
§ 4. [ Secretary of State to Certify to County Clerks
-Manner of Voting ] . It shall be the duty of the Secre-
tary of State to certify , not less than twenty days before
the next general election , to the county clerk of each
county the substance of any such laws , items , sections
or parts of law , which are to be voted upon , and it shall
be the duty of the county clerk to have the same, as cer-
tified by the Secretary of State , indicated on the ballot
and two spaces shall be left upon the right of the same ,
one for votes favoring the measure to be designated by
the word " Yes , " and one for votes opposing the meas-
ure to be designated by the word " No." The elector
shall designate his vote by a cross mark , thus ( X ) ,
placed opposite the word " Yes " or " No. " The votes
cast for and against same shall be counted , canvassed ,
and certified to the State Board of Election Commis-
sioners in the same manner as the votes upon constitu-
tional amendments .
§ 5. [Results of Election-Expenses ] . If it shall be
found that a majority of votes cast for and against such
measure are in favor thereof, then said law , item , sec-
tion or part of law , shall become effective . The result
of such vote shall be published by the Secretary of State
in two daily papers of general circulation published in

40 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
this Commonwealth . The expense of the publications
herein provided for shall be paid as the expense of
other publications which the Secretary of State is re-
quired to make in connection with elections .
§ 6. [Punishment for Illegal Voting ] . If at any
election provided for in this act , any person shall vote
who is not entitled to vote under the laws of this Com-
monwealth at elections for county officers , or if any
person shall sign any petition herein provided for with-
out such qualifications as a voter , or shall impersonate or
sign the name of any other person or persons , he shall be
punished as for a violation of the general laws on elec-
tions . All general laws to protect the purity of elec-
tions shall be applicable to elections held under this act .
Approved May 2, 1917 .

CHAPTER 9 .
AN ACT imposing a license or franchise on any person , firm ,
corporation or association engaged in the production of oil in
this State and authorizing counties also to impose such tax
for road , school and county purposes ; providing methods of
determining the amount of tax due and prescribing penalties
for a violation of the provisions of the act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. [Tax on Oil Production ] . Every person , firm ,
corporation or association engaged in the business of
producing oil in this State , by taking same from the
earth , shall , in lieu of all other taxes on the wells pro-
ducing said oil imposed by law , annually pay a tax for
the right or privilege of engaging in such business in
this State equal to 1 per centum of the market value of
all oil produced in this State , and such tax shall be for
State purposes , and in addition any county in the State
may impose a like tax for road purposes , county pur-

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY . 41
poses or school purposes not to exceed one -half of 1 per
centum of the market value of all oil produced in such
county , and the Fiscal Court of any county may levy
said tax for county purposes and shall determine what
fund or funds shall receive the taxes when collected ,
and when oil is produced in any separate taxing district
in a county the Fiscal Court shall equitably distribute
such taxes between the county and such taxing district .
§ 2. [ Market Value- How Determined ] . The State
Tax Commission , from the reports hereinafter required
to be made and from such other information as it may
obtain , shall determine the fair market value of all oil
produced in this State by any person , firm , corporation
or association , from the date this act goes into effect
until the day on which the first report is required to be
made and thereafter from the day on which the last re-
port was made until the day when the next report is
required to be made . The State Tax Commission shall
determine the fair market value of all oil produced in
any county in this State and shall certify such value to
the County Court Clerk within ten days after such value
has been finally ascertained .
§ 3. [ Notice of Valuation to be Given ] . It shall be
the duty of the State Tax Commission , immediately after
fixing such value to notify the person , firm , corporation
or association of the fact and such person , firm , corpora-
tion or association shall have ten days from the time of
receiving notice to go before said Commission and ask
a change of the valuation and may introduce evidence ,
and said Commission is authorized to summons and
swear witnesses , and after hearing such evidence , the
Commission may change the valuation as it may deem
proper and the action of the Commission shall be final .
§ 4. [When Taxes Due-Penalty ] . All the State
taxes due under the provisions of this act from any per-
son , firm , corporation or association shall be due and

42 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
payable thirty days after notice of same has been given
by the State Tax Commission , and all taxes due to any
county shall be payable thirty days after the certifica-
tion is made to the County Clerk , and every such person ,
firm , corporation or association failing to pay its taxes ,
after receiving thirty days ' notice , shall be deemed de-
linquent and a penalty of ten per cent . on the amount of
tax shall attach and thereafter such tax shall bear in-
terest at the rate of ten per cent . per annum . Any such
person , firm , corporation or association failing to pay
its taxes , penalty and interest , after becoming delin-
quent , shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and on
conviction shall be fined $ 50.00 for each day the same
remains unpaid , to be recovered by indictment or civil
action .
§ 5. [ Certification to Sheriff for Collection ] . It
shall be the duty of the County Clerk , immediately upon
receiving the certification from the State Tax Commis-
sion , to certify same to the Sheriff or collector for col-
lection .
§ 6. [ To Whom Taxes Are Payable ] . All State
taxes due under the provisions of this act shall be paid
to the State Treasurer , and all county taxes shall be paid
to the Sheriff or collector .
[ Reports -Punishment$ 7. For Not Making ] .
Every person , firm , corporation or association engaged
in the production of oil in this State within the mean-
ing of this act shall make a report to the State Tax Com-
mission on the first day of July , 1917 , and every three
months thereafter . The State Tax Commission shall
furnish and prescribe the blanks upon which such re-
ports shall be made . Any person , firm , corporation or
association failing to make such report within thirty
days after same is due , shall be deemed guilty of a mis-
demeanor , and on conviction shall be fined $ 50.00 for

COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY. 43
each day thereafter the report is not made , and such
fine may be recovered by indictment or civil action .
§ 8. [ Pipe Line Companies to Report ] . Every pipe
line company doing business in this State and receiving
oil from any person , firm , corporation or association
shall make a report to the State Tax Commission on the
first day of July , 1917 , and every three months there-
after , showing the quantity of oil received from each
person , firm , corporation or association , and said report
shall be made upon blanks furnished and prescribed by
the State Tax Commission . Any pipe line company fail-
ing to make such report for thirty days after the date
on which same is required to be made shall be deemed
guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon conviction shall be
fined $ 50.00 for each day thereafter that said report is
not made , and said fine may be recovered by indictment
or civil action .
§ 9. [ Blanks to be Prepared by State Tax Commis-
sion ] . The blanks prescribed by the State Tax Com-
mission shall be so prepared as to elicit information such
as will enable the Commission to arrive at the fair
market value of all oil produced in this State and in
the counties in this State , and such report shall be made
by said companies through their chief officer or agent
in this State , and shall be duly verified , and if any such
officer or agent makes a false report he shall be deemed
guilty of false swearing and may be prosecuted .
Approved May 2 , 1917 .
